title: Code Evolution
subtitle: Watch code transform from chaos to clean architecture

stages.label: Scattered
stages.description: Every service creates objects directly. if-else chains everywhere. Adding a type = touching 10+ files.

stages.label: Simple Factory
stages.description: Extract creation into a single class. Clients call factory.create(). One file to change when adding types.
problems: Adding type = modify Factory class (OCP violation)
But only ONE file now, not 10

stages.label: Registry
stages.description: Replace switch with a Map<String, Supplier>. Adding a type = one line registration. Zero modification to existing code.
problems: Still a central registry (single point of config)
But fully OCP compliant — adding = registering, not modifying

stages.label: Factory Method
stages.description: Each service subclass decides which object to create. Framework defines the skeleton, subclasses customize creation.
problems: Class explosion — one subclass per variant
Use when you need polymorphic creation per service type

stages.label: Spring DI
stages.description: Spring's BeanFactory IS a Factory. @Component + @Qualifier replaces all manual creation. The container is the ultimate Factory.
problems: Magic annotations — harder to trace for beginners
But this is how 90% of real Java apps work
